zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vinton於1530年11月20日出生在英國英格蘭艾可瑪格麗特 洛丁時，她的父親Sir John Belstead21歲，母親Mary20歲。她於1553年6月16日與Sir Thomas II Vinton在英國英格蘭艾可維林加爾 多結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1600年在她的家鄉享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vinton于1530年11月20日出生在英国英格兰艾可瑪格麗特 洛丁时，她的父亲Sir John Belstead21岁，母亲Mary20岁。她于1553年6月16日与Sir Thomas II Vinton在英国英格兰艾可維林加爾 多结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1600年在她的家乡享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Belstead
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia and London): habitational name, sometimes from Bensted (Kent) or perhaps Bedlested (in Chelsham, Surrey), but mostly from the manor of Bensted Green (a place now lost, that was in Sandon, Essex). The placenames are probably all from Old English byden stede ‘place in a deep valley or hollow’, although the first element of the Kent name may alternatively be Old English bēonet ‘bent (a kind of long coarse grass)’. Alternatively, some instances may be from Binsted (Hampshire, Sussex), Binstead (Isle of Wight), or Banstead (Surrey), all meaning ‘place where beans grow’ (Old English bēan + stede).

nameMeanings.name: Vinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of   Finton   with voicing of the initial consonant (see   Fenton  ). In Sussex and Kent the name was probably confused with   Winton  .

variants.name: Finton
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Fenton  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vinton was born on 20 November 1530, in Margaret Roding, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Belstead, was 21 and her mother, Mary, was 20. She married Sir Thomas II Vinton on 16 June 1553, in Willingale Doe, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 1600, in her hometown, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vinton est née le 20 novembre 1530, à Margaret Roding, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Belstead, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mary, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas II Vinton le 16 juin 1553, à Willingale Doe, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1600, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans.

Lei sposato Sir Thomas II Vinton il 16 giugno 1553, nel Willingale Doe, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vintonが1530年11月20日にイギリスイギリスエセックスマーガレット ロディングで生まれた時，父Sir John Belsteadは21歳，母Maryは20歳であった。1553年6月16日にイギリスイギリスエセックスウィリンガル ドでSir Thomas II Vintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1600年に故郷で71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vinton(이)가 1530년 11월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 마거릿 로딩에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Belstead(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1553년 6월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 윌링일 도에서 Sir Thomas II Vinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1600년 그녀의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Mary Elizabeth Belstead Vinton родилась 20 ноября 1530 г. в местности маргарет родинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir John Belstead было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mary, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas II Vinton 16 июня 1553 г. в местности уиллингеил до, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1600 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 71 год.
